patient_information.parents_name: Laura Anne Carver and Daniel James Carver
patient_information.address: 4827 Willow Bend Drive, Apt 3B, Hamilton, ON L8P 2Z3

specimen_information.karyotype_if_known: Previous standard karyotype (G-banding) performed at Hamilton General Hospital Cytogenetics Lab in 2023 reported as 46,XY with no visible structural abnormalities; no balanced rearrangements identified at the resolution of conventional karyotyping (approximately 5–10 Mb).

indications_for_testing__family_informat.relationship_to_proband: Proband (index case) – Ethan is the first individual in the family to undergo genomic SNP microarray testing for unexplained global developmental delay and possible neurodevelopmental disorder.
indications_for_testing__family_informat.relevant_family_history:
Ethan is a 6-year-old boy with global developmental delay and suspected autism spectrum disorder. Pregnancy was largely uncomplicated aside from mild maternal gestational hypertension in the third trimester. He was born at 39+2 weeks via spontaneous vaginal delivery, birth weight 3.1 kg (25th percentile), length 50 cm (50th percentile), Apgars 8 and 9 at 1 and 5 minutes. No neonatal ICU admission.

Developmentally, Ethan sat independently at approximately 9–10 months, crawled around 11 months, and walked independently at 20–21 months. First words emerged around 2.5 years, but he has persistent expressive language delay; currently uses mostly 2–3 word phrases with limited spontaneous conversation and some echolalia. Receptive language appears moderately better than expressive. Fine motor skills are delayed (difficulty with buttons, zippers, and using utensils consistently). Occupational therapy notes mild hypotonia and poor motor planning.

He was assessed by a community developmental pediatrician at age 4, who noted limited eye contact, reduced social reciprocity (rarely initiates peer interaction, prefers solitary play, becomes intensely focused on spinning objects and lining up toy cars), sensory sensitivities (covers ears with loud noises, strong aversion to certain clothing textures), and repetitive behaviors (hand-flapping when excited, pacing, insistence on rigid bedtime routine). An ADOS-2 evaluation suggested a profile consistent with autism spectrum disorder. He is currently in a specialized kindergarten program with an Individualized Education Plan, receiving speech-language therapy once weekly and occupational therapy twice monthly.

Family history: Ethan is the first child of non-consanguineous parents of mixed Northern European ancestry (mother: English/Irish; father: Scottish/German). No known consanguinity within the last several generations. There is no known family history of intellectual disability, congenital malformations, or known chromosomal abnormalities. Maternal side: one maternal uncle with childhood attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder, treated medically, now functioning independently as an adult; maternal grandmother has type 2 diabetes and hypertension; maternal grandfather died at 68 of myocardial infarction, no known developmental issues. Paternal side: one paternal cousin diagnosed with dyslexia and mild learning difficulties requiring resource support in school but completed high school; paternal grandfather has Parkinson’s disease with onset in his late 60s; paternal grandmother has hypothyroidism. No recurrent miscarriages reported (mother: one early first-trimester miscarriage at ~7 weeks prior to Ethan, no products of conception tested). No family history of autism spectrum disorder, epilepsy, known genetic syndromes (e.g., Fragile X, Down syndrome), or early-onset neurodegenerative disease.

Previous investigations for Ethan include normal newborn metabolic screen, normal thyroid function tests, normal serum lactate and ammonia, and normal hearing assessment by audiology. A brain MRI at age 4 showed mildly delayed myelination but no focal structural malformations, no evidence of cortical dysplasia, and no significant white matter lesions. Fragile X testing (FMR1 CGG repeat analysis) performed in 2023 was negative (normal repeat range). Standard karyotype (46,XY) did not reveal any visible chromosomal deletions, duplications, or balanced rearrangements at the routine resolution. Given persistent global developmental delay, behavioral features consistent with autism spectrum disorder, and lack of a unifying diagnosis, genomic SNP microarray is being pursued to look for pathogenic copy number variants (e.g., microdeletions or microduplications) or regions of homozygosity that might explain his neurodevelopmental phenotype.

Parents are particularly concerned about recurrence risk for future pregnancies (they are considering a second child) and about whether any identified genetic change may have implications for extended family members. They have expressed interest in understanding the full range of potential outcomes of testing (pathogenic, likely pathogenic, VUS, or incidental findings) and have met with a genetic counselor to discuss these issues in detail.
indications_for_testing__family_informat.pedigree_at_least_3_generation_when_avai:
Three-generation pedigree (described in text):

Generation I:
- Maternal grandparents: MGM (age 67), history of type 2 diabetes and hypertension; no known developmental delays or congenital anomalies. MGF (deceased at 68 from myocardial infarction), no reported learning or developmental issues.
- Paternal grandparents: PGM (age 70), hypothyroidism, otherwise healthy, no known neurodevelopmental conditions. PGF (age 72), Parkinson’s disease diagnosed in late 60s, no childhood developmental concerns.

Generation II:
- Mother: Laura Anne Carver (age 34), healthy, completed university, works as a high school English teacher. No history of developmental delay, learning disability, or psychiatric hospitalization. One early miscarriage at ~7 weeks before Ethan.
- Maternal siblings: one brother (maternal uncle, age 32) with childhood ADHD, treated with stimulant medication, currently employed full time; no intellectual disability. No congenital anomalies.
- Father: Daniel James Carver (age 36), healthy, completed college, works as a mechanical engineering technologist. No developmental delay or learning disability.
- Paternal siblings: one sister (paternal aunt, age 38), healthy; her son (paternal cousin, age 10) has dyslexia and mild learning difficulties requiring educational accommodations but no global developmental delay.

Generation III:
- Proband: Ethan Michael Carver (2019-03-14), male, global developmental delay and suspected autism spectrum disorder as described.
- No full or half-siblings. No known congenital anomalies, intellectual disability, or autism reported among first cousins apart from the one cousin with dyslexia on the paternal side.

No reported consanguinity. No known history of recurrent pregnancy loss, neonatal deaths, or multiple family members with similar neurodevelopmental presentations. This pedigree suggests a largely sporadic presentation in the proband, though subtle learning/attention issues in extended relatives raise the possibility of a heritable susceptibility variant; SNP microarray is being used as a first-tier test to evaluate for pathogenic CNVs or regions of homozygosity that could clarify etiology and recurrence risk.
